     CompletableFuture<DescribeShareGroupOffsetsResponseData.DescribeShareGroupOffsetsResponseGroup> responseFuture,\\n1811:         String groupId\\n1812:     ) {\\n1813:         // This set keeps Thus, the call to join() here is safe.\\n1873:                             PartitionMetadataClient.OffsetResponse offsetResponse = partitionLatestOffsets.get(tp).join();\\n1874:                             if (offsetResponse.error().code() != Errors.NONE.code()) {\\n1875:                                 // If there was an error during fetching latest offset for a partition, return the error in the response for that partition.\\n1876:                                 log.error(\\\"Partition end offset fetch failed for topicPartition {}\\\", tp);\\n1877:                                 partitionResponses.add(new DescribeShareGroupOffsetsResponseData.DescribeShareGroupOffsetsResponsePartition()\\n1878:                                     .setPartitionIndex(partitionData.partition())\\n1879:                                     .setErrorCode(offsetResponse.error().code())\\n1880:                                     .setErrorMessage(offsetResponse.error().message()));\\n1881:                             } else {\\n1882:                                 // Compute lag as (partition end offset - startOffset - deliveryCompleteCount).\\n1883:                                 // Note, partition end offset, which is retrieved from partitionMetadataClient, is the offset of\\n1884:                                 // the next message to be produced, not the last message offset. partitionData.startOffset() - partitionData.deliveryCompleteCount();\\n1887:                                 partitionResponses.add(new                 .setStartOffset(partitionData.startOffset())\\n1890:                                     .setLeaderEpoch(partitionData.leaderEpoch())\\n1891: @Override\n1606:     public CompletableFuture<OffsetFetchResponseData.OffsetFetchResponseGroup> fetchOffsets(\n1607:         AuthorizableRequestContext context,\n1608: partitionData.partition()));\n1821:                 }\n1822:             });\n1823:         });\n1824: \n1825:         // Fetch latest offsets for all partitions that need lag computation.\n1826: This is just a safety check.\n1842:                 if (error != null) {\n1843:                     log.error(\"Failed to retrieve partition end offsets while calculating share partitions lag for share group - {}\", groupId, error);\n1844:                     responseFuture.completeExceptionally(error);\n1845:                     return;\n1846:                 }\n1847:                 readSummaryResult.topicsData().forEach(topicData -> {\n1848:                     // Build response for each topic.\n1849:                     DescribeShareGroupOffsetsResponseData.DescribeShareGroupOffsetsResponseTopic topic =\n1850:                         new DescribeShareGroupOffsetsResponseData.DescribeShareGroupOffsetsResponseTopic()\n1851:                             .setTopicId(topicData.topicId())\n1852:                             .setTopicName(requestTopicIdToNameMapping.get(topicData.topicId()));\n1853: \n1854:                     // Build response for each partition within the topic.\n1855:                     List<DescribeShareGroupOffsetsResponseData.DescribeShareGroupOffsetsResponsePartition> partitionResponses = new ArrayList<>();\n1856: \n1857:                     topicData.partitions().forEach(partitionData -> {\n1858:                         TopicPartition tp = new TopicPartition(requestTopicIdToNameMapping.get(topicData.topicId()), partitionData.partition());\n1859:                         // For the partitions where lag computation is not needed, a partitionResponse is built directly.\n1860:                         // The lag is set to -1 (uninitialized lag) in these cases. If the persister returned an error for a\n1861:                         // partition, the startOffset is set to -1 (uninitialized offset) and the leaderEpoch is set to 0\n1862:                         // (default epoch). This is consistent with OffsetFetch for situations in which there is no offset\n1863:                         // information to fetch. It's treated as absence of data, rather than an error\n1864:                         if (!shouldComputeSharePartitionLag(partitionData)) {\n1865:                             partitionResponses.add(new DescribeShareGroupOffsetsResponseData.DescribeShareGroupOffsetsResponsePartition()\n1866:                                 .setPartitionIndex(partitionData.partition())\n1867:                                 .setStartOffset(partitionData.errorCode() == Errors.NONE.code() ? partitionData.startOffset() : PartitionFactory.UNINITIALIZED_START_OFFSET)\n1868:                                 .setLeaderEpoch(partitionData.errorCode() == Errors.NONE.code() ? partitionData.leaderEpoch() : PartitionFactory.DEFAULT_LEADER_EPOCH)\n1869:                                 .setLag(PartitionFactory.UNINITIALIZED_LAG));\n1870:                         } else {\n1871:                             // This code is reached when allOf above is complete, which happens when all the\n1872:                             // individual futures are complete. Thus, the call to join() here is safe.\n1873:                             PartitionMetadataClient.OffsetResponse offsetResponse = partitionLatestOffsets.get(tp).join();\n1874:                             if (offsetResponse.error().code() != Errors.NONE.code()) {\n1875:                                 // If there was an error during fetching latest offset for a partition, return the error in the response for that partition.\n1876:                                 log.error(\"Partition end offset fetch failed for topicPartition {}\", tp);\n1877:                                 partitionResponses.add(new DescribeShareGroupOffsetsResponseData.DescribeShareGroupOffsetsResponsePartition()\n1878:                                     .setPartitionIndex(partitionData.partition())\n1879:                                     .setErrorCode(offsetResponse.error().code())\n1880:                                     .setErrorMessage(offsetResponse.error().message()));\n1881:                             } else {\n1882:                                 // Compute lag as (partition end offset - startOffset - deliveryCompleteCount).\n1883:                                 // Note, partition end offset, which is retrieved from partitionMetadataClient, is the offset of\n1884:                                 // the next message to be produced, not the last message offset. Thus, the formula for lag computation\n1885:                                 // does not need a +1 adjustment.\n1886:                                 long lag = offsetResponse.offset() - partitionData.startOffset() - partitionData.deliveryCompleteCount();\n1887:                                 partitionResponses.add(new DescribeShareGroupOffsetsResponseData.DescribeShareGroupOffsetsResponsePartition()\n1888:                                     .setPartitionIndex(partitionData.partition())\n1889:                                     .setStartOffset(partitionData.startOffset())\n1890:                                     .setLeaderEpoch(partitionData.leaderEpoch())\n1891:                                     .setLag(lag));\n1892:                             }\n1893:                         }\n1894:                     });\n1895:                     topic.setPartitions(partitionResponses);\n1896:                     responseTopics.add(topic);\n1897:                 });\n1898: \n1899:                 // Add topics which did not exist in the metadata image and were handled earlier.\n1900:                 responseTopics.addAll(describeShareGroupOffsetsResponseTopicList);\n1901: ","url":"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kafka--e678b4b@47ac12bd6042edf23f1dbca3d789db945d62802d/-/blob/group-coordinator/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/coordinator/group/GroupCoordinatorService.java%3FL1600-1900"}}}

The service handles\nconsumer group heartbeats, offset commits, group lifecycle (join/sync/leave/delete), share-group\nstate, and coordinator partition leadership changes.\n\nAfter reading both files in full, seven distinct untested failure modes were identified. The most\ncritical is that the non-transactional `commitOffsets` path has **zero test coverage** despite being\nthe primary offset-commit code path for all classic and consumer-group clients. The remaining gaps\ninvolve missing validation edge cases, complex async failure chains in share-group state management,\nand an unguarded race in the classic-group sync/join response-future lifecycle.\n\n---\n\n## Failure Modes and Proposed Tests\n\n### Failure Mode 1: `commitOffsets` — entire method is untested\n\n- **Risk**: HIGH\n- **Code Location**: `GroupCoordinatorService.java` ~line 2062\n- **Description**: The `commitOffsets` method (invoked for every `OFFSET_COMMIT` API call from\n  classic and modern consumer groups) has **no tests at all** in `GroupCoordinatorServiceTest`.\n  This includes the not-started guard (`COORDINATOR_NOT_AVAILABLE`), the null-groupId guard\n  (`INVALID_GROUP_ID`), the happy-path write-operation dispatch, and the exception mapping via\n  `handleOperationException`. By contrast, the analogous `commitTransactionalOffsets` method\n  has five tests. .setPartitions(List.of(new OffsetCommitRequestData.OffsetCommitRequestPartition()\n                  .setPartitionIndex(0)\n                  .setCommittedOffset(42L)))));\n\n response.topics().get(0).partitions().get(0).errorCode());\n  }\n\n  @Test\n  public void testCommitOffsetsWithNullGroupId() throws Exception {\n      GroupCoordinatorService service = new — the `isActive` guard, the null-groupId guard,\n  the `scheduleWriteOperation(\"commit-offset\", …)` dispatch, and the `exceptionally` handler. This\n  is currently 0 % covered for the `commitOffsets` method.\n\n---\n\n### Failure Mode 2: `heartbeat` — missing `INVALID_GROUP_ID` validation\n\n- **Risk**: MEDIUM\n- **Code Location**: `GroupCoordinatorService.java` ~line 1011\n- **Description**: `heartbeat` calls `isGroupIdNotEmpty(request.groupId())` at line 1011 and\n  returns `INVALID_GROUP_ID` when the group ID is null or blank. The analogous paths are tested for\n  `joinGroup`, `syncGroup`, `deleteOffsets`, and `leaveGroup`, but the four existing `heartbeat`\n  tests (`testHeartbeat`, `testHeartbeatCoordinatorNotAvailableException`,\n  `testHeartbeatCoordinatorException`, `testHeartbeatWhenNotStarted`) all use a non-empty group ID.\n  A null or empty group ID will silently pass the `isActive` guard and hit an untested branch.\n- **Proposed Test**:\n  ```java\n  @ParameterizedTest\n  @NullSource\n  @ValueSource(strings = {\"\"})\n  public void testHeartbeatWithInvalidGroupId(String groupId)\n      throws ExecutionException, InterruptedException {\n      GroupCoordinatorService service = new GroupCoordinatorServiceBuilder()\n          .setConfig(createConfig())\n          .setRuntime(mockRuntime())\n          .build(true);\n\n      HeartbeatRequestData request = new HeartbeatRequestData()\n          .setGroupId(groupId);\n\n      CompletableFuture<HeartbeatResponseData> future =\n          service.heartbeat(requestContext(ApiKeys.HEARTBEAT), request);\n\n      assertTrue(future.isDone());\n      assertEquals(Errors.INVALID_GROUP_ID.code(), future.get().errorCode());\n  }\n  ```\n- **Coverage Delta**: Exercises the `isGroupIdNotEmpty` early-return branch at lines 1011–1015.\n  Currently the false branch of that `if` is never reached in this test class.\n\n---\n\n### Failure Mode 3: `leaveGroup` — missing `INVALID_GROUP_ID` validation\n\n- **Risk**: MEDIUM\n- **Code Location**: `GroupCoordinatorService.java` ~line 1053\n- **Description**: `leaveGroup` checks `isGroupIdNotEmpty` at line 1053 and returns\n  `INVALID_GROUP_ID` for null/blank group IDs. Three tests exist for `leaveGroup`\n  (`testLeaveGroup`, `testLeaveGroupThrowsUnknownMemberIdException`,\n  `testLeaveGroupWhenNotStarted`), but none tests the invalid-group-ID path. A client with an\n  improperly configured group ID would receive an `INVALID_GROUP_ID` error, and this is\n  unverified.\n- **Proposed Test**:\n  ```java\n  @ParameterizedTest\n  @NullSource\n  @ValueSource(strings = {\"\"})\n  public void testLeaveGroupWithInvalidGroupId(String groupId)\n      throws ExecutionException, InterruptedException {\n      GroupCoordinatorService service = new GroupCoordinatorServiceBuilder()\n          .setConfig(createConfig())\n          .setRuntime(mockRuntime())\n          .build(true);\n\n      LeaveGroupRequestData request = new LeaveGroupRequestData()\n          .setGroupId(groupId);\n\n      CompletableFuture<LeaveGroupResponseData> future =\n          service.leaveGroup(requestContext(ApiKeys.LEAVE_GROUP), request);\n\n      assertTrue(future.isDone());\n      assertEquals(Errors.INVALID_GROUP_ID.code(), future.get().errorCode());\n  }\n  ```\n- **Coverage Delta**: Exercises the `isGroupIdNotEmpty` early-return branch at lines 1053–1057,\n  currently 0 % covered for `leaveGroup`.\n\n---\n\n### Failure Mode 4: `persisterInitialize(ShareGroupHeartbeatResponseData)` — persister failure\n  cascades into `uninitializeShareGroupState` which itself fails\n\n- **Risk**: HIGH\n- **Code Location**: `GroupCoordinatorService.java` ~lines 808–827 and 861–879\n- **Description**: When persister initialization fails inside\n  `persisterInitialize(InitializeShareGroupStateParameters, ShareGroupHeartbeatResponseData)`,\n  the code calls `uninitializeShareGroupState` (line 824) to roll back the share-group state.\n  `uninitializeShareGroupState` schedules a write operation and has its own `exceptionally` handler\n  (lines 873–879) that returns an error `ShareGroupHeartbeatResponseData` with\n  `UNKNOWN_SERVER_ERROR`. If *that* write also fails, the error is returned to the caller. No test\n  exercises this double-failure scenario: persister init failure → uninitialize write failure. The\n  `exceptionally` block at line 873 is never triggered in the test suite.\n- **Proposed Test**:\n  ```java\n  @Test\n  public void Failure Mode 5: `deleteShareGroupOffsetsState` — `persister.deleteState` throws an exception\n\n- **Risk**: MEDIUM\n- **Code Location**: `GroupCoordinatorService.java` ~lines 1985–1990\n- **Description**: In `deleteShareGroupOffsetsState`, after building the persister request,\n  `persister.deleteState(...)` is called at line 1985. If the future completes exceptionally, the\n  `exceptionally` handler at line 1987–1990 returns an error `DeleteShareGroupOffsetsResponseData`\n  via `getErrorDeleteResponseData`. The existing test\n  `testDeleteShareGroupOffsetsRequestThrowsError` only covers the case where the *first* write\n  operation (`initiate-delete-share-group-offsets`) fails; there is no test where that write\n  succeeds, returns a valid `DeleteShareGroupOffsetsResultHolder` with a non-null\n  `deleteStateRequestParameters`, but then `persister.deleteState` fails.\n- **Proposed Test**:\n  ```java\n  @Test\n  public void testDeleteShareGroupOffsetsStateWhenPersisterDeleteStateFails()\n      throws ExecutionException, InterruptedException {\n      CoordinatorRuntime<GroupCoordinatorShard, CoordinatorRecord> runtime = mockRuntime();\n      Persister persister = mock(DefaultStatePersister.class);\n      GroupCoordinatorService service = new `persister.deleteState(...).exceptionally(...)` branch at\n  lines 1987–1990, and the error-response builder `getErrorDeleteResponseData(Errors.forException(throwable))`.\n\n---\n\n### Failure Mode 6: `fetchOffsets` — null group ID returns `INVALID_GROUP_ID`\n\n- **Risk**: MEDIUM\n- **Code Location**: `GroupCoordinatorService.java` ~line 1619\n- **Description**: `fetchOffsets` has a specific null-check for `request.groupId()` at lines\n  1619–1626 (distinct from empty string, for backward compatibility). While\n  `testFetchOffsetsWhenNotStarted` and `testFetchOffsets` exercise other paths, there is no test\n  that passes a null group ID to `fetchOffsets` on an active service. The code path at line\n  1620–1625 returns `OffsetFetchResponse.groupError(request, INVALID_GROUP_ID, ...)` — this\n  conditional is never executed under test.\n- **Proposed Test**:\n  ```java\n  @ParameterizedTest\n  @CsvSource({\"false\", \"true\"})\n  public void testFetchOffsetsWithNullGroupId(boolean requireStable)\n      throws ExecutionException, InterruptedException {\n      GroupCoordinatorService service = new GroupCoordinatorServiceBuilder()\n          .setConfig(createConfig())\n          .setRuntime(mockRuntime())\n          .build(true);\n\n      OffsetFetchRequestData.OffsetFetchRequestGroup request =\n          new OffsetFetchRequestData.OffsetFetchRequestGroup()\n              .setGroupId(null);  // triggers the null-check at line 1620\n\n      CompletableFuture<OffsetFetchResponseData.OffsetFetchResponseGroup> future =\n          service.fetchOffsets(requestContext(ApiKeys.OFFSET_FETCH), request, requireStable);\n\n      assertTrue(future.isDone());\n      assertEquals(Errors.INVALID_GROUP_ID.code(), future.get().errorCode());\n  }\n  ```\n- **Coverage Delta**: Exercises the `request.groupId() == null` guard at lines 1619–1626, which\n  returns an `INVALID_GROUP_ID` group-error response. The branch is independent of `requireStable`\n  so both values should be covered.\n\n---\n\n### Failure Mode 7: `joinGroup`/`syncGroup` — exception swallowed when `responseFuture` is already done\n\n- **Risk**: LOW\n- **Code Location**: `GroupCoordinatorService.java` ~lines 938–948 (`joinGroup`) and 981–992\n  (`syncGroup`)\n- **Description**: Both `joinGroup` and `syncGroup` use a separate `responseFuture` that is\n  completed by the coordinator shard (not by the write operation's return value). The\n  `exceptionally` handler checks `if (!responseFuture.isDone())` before completing the future with\n  the error. If the coordinator has already completed `responseFuture` (e.g., because the member\n  was assigned) before the `scheduleWriteOperation` future itself fails, the exception is silently\n  discarded — no log, no error propagation. This guard has no test coverage: neither\n  `testJoinGroupWithException` nor `testSyncGroupWithException` sets up `responseFuture` as\n  already done when the exception fires.\n- **Proposed Test**:\n  ```java\n  @Test\n  public void testJoinGroupExceptionIgnoredWhenResponseFutureAlreadyDone() throws Exception {\n      CoordinatorRuntime<GroupCoordinatorShard, CoordinatorRecord> runtime = mockRuntime();\n      GroupCoordinatorService service = new GroupCoordinatorServiceBuilder()\n          .setConfig(createConfig())\n          .setRuntime(runtime)\n          .build(true);\n\n      JoinGroupRequestData request = new JoinGroupRequestData()\n          .setGroupId(\"foo\")\n          .setSessionTimeoutMs(1000);\n\n      JoinGroupResponseData successResponse = new JoinGroupResponseData()\n          .setMemberId(\"member-1\")\n          .setGenerationId(1);\n\n      // The write operation returns a CompletableFuture that fails, but BEFORE failing it\n      // simulates the shard having already completed responseFuture with a successful response.\n      // We inject this by capturing the CoordinatorWriteOperation and completing its future\n      // directly, then failing the outer future.\n      CompletableFuture<JoinGroupResponseData> outerFuture =\n          new CompletableFuture<>();\n      when(runtime.scheduleWriteOperation(\n          ArgumentMatchers.eq(\"classic-group-join\"),\n          ArgumentMatchers.any(),\n          ArgumentMatchers.any()\n      )).thenAnswer(invocation -> {\n          // Simulate shard completing responseFuture before the outer future resolves\n          outerFuture.completeExceptionally(new IllegalStateException(\"write failed\"));\n          return outerFuture;\n      });\n\n      // Override behaviour: when the shard calls responseFuture.complete, the future is\n      // already completed with successResponse. Here we verify the service future returns\n      // the pre-completed success value, not an error.\n      // (This test relies on ability to pre-seed the inner future; adapt to mock setup.)\n      CompletableFuture<JoinGroupResponseData> serviceFuture = service.joinGroup(\n          requestContext(ApiKeys.JOIN_GROUP), request, BufferSupplier.NO_CACHING);\n\n      // responseFuture should NOT have been re-completed with an error\n      // (The if (!responseFuture.isDone()) guard at line 939 protects it.)\n      // The future will resolve without throwing.\n      assertDoesNotThrow(() -> serviceFuture.get(5, TimeUnit.SECONDS));\n  }\n  ```\n- **Coverage Delta**: Exercises the `if (!responseFuture.isDone())` guard at line 939 (and the\n  analogous guard at line 982 for `syncGroup`). Currently neither branch of that condition is\n  exercised in the test suite.\n\n---\n\n## File References\n\n| File | Role |\n|------|------|\n| `group-coordinator/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/coordinator/group/GroupCoordinatorService.java` | Implementation under analysis |\n| `group-coordinator/src/test/java/org/apache/kafka/coordinator/group/GroupCoordinatorServiceTest.java` | Existing test suite |\n| `group-coordinator/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/coordinator/group/GroupCoordinator.java` | Interface definition |\n| `group-coordinator/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/coordinator/group/GroupCoordinatorShard.java` | Shard-level logic delegated to by the service |\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":3,"cache_creation_input_tokens":771,"cache_read_input_tokens":144517,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":771,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":5,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"2451af0c-5c17-4d29-ad28-edbb339ce0bc","uuid":"0b23160a-4e6b-4bf3-a47b-e54bc6c66143"}

The service handles\nconsumer group heartbeats, offset commits, group lifecycle (join/sync/leave/delete), share-group\nstate, and coordinator partition leadership changes.\n\nAfter reading both files in full, seven distinct untested failure modes were identified. The most\ncritical is that the non-transactional `commitOffsets` path has **zero test coverage** despite being\nthe primary offset-commit code path for all classic and consumer-group clients. The remaining gaps\ninvolve missing validation edge cases, complex async failure chains in share-group state management,\nand an unguarded race in the classic-group sync/join response-future lifecycle.\n\n---\n\n## Failure Modes and Proposed Tests\n\n### Failure Mode 1: `commitOffsets` — entire method is untested\n\n- **Risk**: HIGH\n- **Code Location**: `GroupCoordinatorService.java` ~line 2062\n- **Description**: The `commitOffsets` method (invoked for every `OFFSET_COMMIT` API call from\n  classic and modern consumer groups) has **no tests at all** in `GroupCoordinatorServiceTest`.\n  This includes the not-started guard (`COORDINATOR_NOT_AVAILABLE`), the null-groupId guard\n  (`INVALID_GROUP_ID`), the happy-path write-operation dispatch, and the exception mapping via\n  `handleOperationException`.
